title: orthocarpus imbricatus
intro: Orthocarpus imbricatus, also known as the brick mullein or rock mullein, is a flowering plant species in the figwort family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Orthocarpus imbricatus are said to include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to soothe skin irritations and wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often dried and used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
